entry.description: A remote code execution vulnerability exists in the API gateway due to unsafe deserialization of untrusted XML payloads. Attackers can trigger arbitrary command execution without authentication by sending a crafted HTTP POST request.
Path traversal in the file export handler allows an authenticated attacker to read sensitive server files by manipulating user-controlled paths using ../ sequences.
Insufficient rate limiting in the login service could enable brute-force credential attacks at scale allowing unlimited password guesses against any user account.
SQL injection vulnerability in the user search parameter of the admin panel allows unauthenticated attackers to read, modify, and delete database records due to insufficient input sanitization.
Reflected XSS vulnerability in the comment field of user profile pages. Requires user interaction and impacts only the victim browser session within the same origin.
Authentication bypass via JWT token signature validation flaw. Attackers can forge tokens exploiting algorithm confusion between RS256 and HS256, gaining unauthorized access to protected endpoints.
Server-side request forgery in the webhook configuration endpoint allows authenticated users to make requests to internal network services and private metadata APIs.

OS command injection in the network diagnostics tool allows authenticated administrators to execute arbitrary commands on the host. Input to the ping utility is unsanitized, enabling shell metacharacter injection.
Insecure direct object reference in the document download endpoint allows authenticated users to access documents of other users by manipulating the document ID in the request URL.

Missing CSRF protection on state-changing API endpoints allows attackers to trick authenticated users into performing unintended actions including account changes and data deletion via crafted web pages.
